Observe the Paper Lanterns    Select the true statement from the choices below and follow its instructions:    A red lantern is always next to a yellow lantern.    The drop is inside the house that's located down the street on the right. Look behind the red armchair on the second floor.  ---  There are more than nineteen yellow lanterns but fewer than twenty-two.    Continue down the street. Turn right and pass through the garden arch. Turn right again and continue straight down the street. Look behind the last crates.  ---  Blue lanterns are the rarest of them all. Backtrack toward the location of the first note. Go down the stairs on your right. Once down them, look to the right of the door.    —CB

Of all places to build a base, the Arcane Eye chose here. Just who are they keeping an eye on? Tourists? It is still odd seeing these people live out their lives. Somehow it feels wrong to be watching them like this. A total violation of privacy... But this might be my bias talking. Before I left Rata Sum, they considered me a battery without any juice. Well, I feel the same about them.  I don't know what Isgarren is hoping to achieve with these fractals, but it's got to be benevolent, right? Maybe Mabon could put my mind at ease about this... It all just seems so surreal to be the one watching.

Of the many weapons within our enemies' arsenal, spectral agony continues to prove itself the most dangerous and terrible of them all.    Not only does it inflict the most excruciating pain imaginable without causing a single wound, but it also saps the victim's will to fight against it, crushing down upon them until they can no longer think of anything but the pain.    That the mursaat would even devise such a spell speaks volumes about their capacity for cruelty and savagery, and it gives our people every reason to oppose them at every turn. There can be no reasoning with such beings, no peace that can be brokered when such methods are employed.    This council would do well to heed my call on this matter, even in my exile, lest we find ourselves groveling before these foul beings and their ultimate weapon of control. We must act, and act now.    There can be no compromise on this.    —Isgarren
